Find Sherpas Boutique Search Agency
Search Quality Report

60 Huckberry Searches. 34 Revenue-Risk Moments.

An independent audit of how Huckberry search handles product names, brands, filters, and shopper intent.
Prepared by Michal Pekarcik · Find Sherpas
Report Crossroads

Jump to a section

Search risk at a glance → Tested query patterns → Improvement map → Advanced diagnostics → Prioritized roadmap → Revenue-risk failures → Detailed analysis → Complete test results → Scope and boundaries →
Summary

Search risk at a glance

The short version

We tested 60 realistic customer queries against Huckberry's search, live on site. The audit checks whether customers see a relevant top result, not just whether the search engine returns something.

This report focuses on the 34 queries where a customer likely hits a dead end: an off-topic result, a missing interpretation, a dropped constraint, or a relevant product buried too far down.

The first fix to ship: Put query intent rules in front of generic keyword ranking — This gives Product Discovery, Brand Search, Typo Tolerance, and Shopping Context a concrete first layer: route sale and bestseller terms to the right collections, apply price and negative filters, and boost recognized brands or product types before broad keyword matches take over.

57%
revenue risk. 34 of 60 tested queries cause a likely customer bounce today. These are queries where a customer sees a dead end: no relevant top result, wrong interpretation, or the right product buried too far down.
34
Revenue risk
Off-topic top result, missing interpretation, or severe ranking failure. The customer leaves.
21
Friction
The query is close, but still creates work: the best match is not surfaced cleanly enough.
5
Working well
Top result is relevant and ranking is near-optimal. No immediate attention needed.
60%
Right products found, ranked too low
#6
Avg. best result position
53%
Best result outside top 3
29%
Irrelevant #1 result
Most severe example A customer searching "Flint adn Tinder" gets zero results — a blank page — even though you carry it (e.g. "Flint and Tinder x Rancourt & Co. Leather Tote" shows up when they instead search "Flint and Tinder").
Pattern Calibration Map

Where each query family needs tuning

These are diagnostic probes, not grades. Each row shows the strength of the tuning signal in that sample, separating severe shopper-visible misses from minor ranking polish.

Product Discovery
Direct match
Exact-match polish
4 probes reviewed: 4 polish notes.
The pattern mostly surfaces relevant products; the remaining work is ranking polish.
Example “The Apres Short - 7"” — The search correctly returned 6 identical 'The Apres Short - 7"' products with perfect relevance scores (0.914), but ranked promotional content incorrectly…
Can customers find an exact product when they already know the name?
Next tuning move: Protect exact product-title matches and verify they are not outranked by broader keyword matches.
Product Discovery
Broad category
Collection routing
4 probes reviewed: 2 visible misses, 2 polish notes.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “bestsellers” — The query 'bestsellers' should map to a bestsellers category/collection, but instead the engine treated it as a keyword search.
Does search understand high-level shopping terms and collection-style queries?
Next tuning move: Map common retail terms to collections, category pages, and merchandising rules.
Product Discovery
Category mapping
Taxonomy mapping
4 probes reviewed: 3 visible misses, 1 polish note.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “casual wear” — The highest relevance results were buried deep in rankings: Transport Casual Jacket (0.621 relevance) appeared at positions #13 and #14, while much lower…
Can colloquial customer language map to the site's actual merchandising categories?
Next tuning move: Create a synonym layer between customer phrases and internal taxonomy labels.
Typo Tolerance
Typo
Typo recovery
4 probes reviewed: 2 visible misses, 1 friction point, 1 stable result.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “Flint adn Tinder” — Query 'Flint adn Tinder' returned zero results despite containing only a simple typo ('adn' instead of 'and').
Does search recover when brand or product terms are misspelled?
Next tuning move: Add fuzzy matching for high-value brands, product names, and common category terms.
Typo Tolerance
Partial query
Autocomplete intent
4 probes reviewed: 2 visible misses, 2 polish notes.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “365 Chin” — The highest relevance result '365 Chino Short - 5' (0.676) was buried at position #12, while lower relevance pants dominated the top positions.
Do incomplete typed-ahead queries surface plausible products before the full term is entered?
Next tuning move: Tune prefix matching and autocomplete-backed retrieval for short or incomplete terms.
Language Understanding
Synonym
Vocabulary mapping
4 probes reviewed: 3 visible misses, 1 polish note.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “linen button up” — The highest relevance result 'Classic Linen Shirt' (0.629) was buried at position #13, while much lower relevance results dominated the top positions: 'Linen…
Does search connect customer vocabulary to the store's preferred product wording?
Next tuning move: Expand synonym dictionaries and semantic embeddings for the product language customers actually use.
Language Understanding
Natural language
Intent translation
4 probes reviewed: 4 visible misses.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “need something for outdoor activities” — The search engine returned zero results for the query 'need something for outdoor activities' despite this being a natural language request that should map to…
Can longer, conversational needs be translated into product intent?
Next tuning move: Extract intent phrases from longer queries before ranking products.
Brand & Model Search
Brand search
Brand protection
4 probes reviewed: 3 visible misses, 1 polish note.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “Relwen Flyweight” — The highest relevance products (Flyweight Flex Short - 8.5" with 0.539 relevance) were buried at positions #10-15, while lower relevance products (Flyweight…
Are brand requests recognized and protected from unrelated results?
Next tuning move: Detect brand entities and boost or filter to products from the requested brand.
Filters & Constraints
Facet extraction
Attribute parsing
4 probes reviewed: 1 visible miss, 1 friction point, 1 polish note, 1 stable result.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “7 inch shorts” — The highest relevance products '7" Training Short' (0.867 relevance) were buried at positions #11-13, while lower relevance products dominated the top positions.
Are structured attributes like material, size, or product feature extracted from text?
Next tuning move: Parse attributes into structured facets, then enforce them in ranking or filtering.
Filters & Constraints
Negative intent
Exclusion handling
4 probes reviewed: 3 visible misses, 1 stable result.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “shirts not linen” — The query 'shirts not linen' explicitly excludes linen shirts, but 14 out of 15 results are linen shirts: positions #1-9 and #11-15 all show 'Short Sleeve…
Can the engine respect exclusions such as 'not', 'without', or 'no'?
Next tuning move: Add exclusion handling so banned terms reduce or remove matching products.
Filters & Constraints
Multi-attribute
Constraint stacking
4 probes reviewed: 1 visible miss, 1 polish note, 2 stable results.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “Proof sport shorts sale” — The search returned zero results for the query 'Proof sport shorts sale' despite the query containing valid search terms.
Can search keep multiple requirements active at the same time?
Next tuning move: Score each constraint independently so one matched term does not drown out the rest.
Filters & Constraints
Price anchored
Price parsing
4 probes reviewed: 3 visible misses, 1 polish note.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “sale shirts under 60” — The search returned zero results for 'sale shirts under 60' despite the displacement metric indicating the best result was at position #16, suggesting relevant…
Can price constraints be recognized and used as a ranking or filtering signal?
Next tuning move: Parse numeric price limits and connect them to product price metadata.
Shopping Context
Plural / singular
Term normalization
4 probes reviewed: 3 visible misses, 1 friction point.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “short” — The two highest relevance products 'Cord Supply Short - 7.5"' (0.719 relevance) were ranked at positions #11 and #12 instead of #1 and #2.
Does search normalize simple wording variants such as singular and plural terms?
Next tuning move: Normalize singular, plural, and close inflection variants before retrieval.
Shopping Context
Subjective attribute
Merchandising language
4 probes reviewed: 2 visible misses, 2 friction points.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “versatile summer shirts” — The highest relevance result 'Tropical Camo Short Sleeve Shirt' (0.461) was buried at position #14, while the lowest relevance result 'Tellaro Sweater Polo'…
Can descriptive shopping language like premium, casual, or comfortable be mapped sensibly?
Next tuning move: Connect subjective descriptors to curated tags, product copy, and merchandising attributes.
Shopping Context
Seasonal / occasion
Occasion matching
4 probes reviewed: 2 visible misses, 1 friction point, 1 polish note.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “spring sale shorts” — The search returned zero results for 'spring sale shorts' despite this being a straightforward seasonal product query.
Can seasonal or occasion-based needs map to relevant categories and products?
Next tuning move: Add occasion and season tags, then use them as ranking signals for broad needs.

Advanced Diagnostics

What the visible result set reveals

These checks stay inside the same query → results → audit boundary, but look beyond the single best result.

Result Set Purity

This checks how much of the visible result set is actually relevant, not just whether one good result exists somewhere. A noisy top 5 means customers must visually filter around weak results even when search technically found a match.

36%
top 5 relevant

Result

The visible result set is noisy: only 36% of top 5 results were relevant on average, and top 10 purity was 35%.

Evidence

  • "active": 0/5 relevant in top 5
  • "bestsellers": 0/5 relevant in top 5
  • "workout gear": 0/5 relevant in top 5
  • "casual wear": 0/5 relevant in top 5

How to remedy it

Tune ranking to demote low-relevance partial matches once a strong intent match exists. Add a top-N quality guardrail: for each important query class, require a minimum share of relevant results in the top 5, not only a good best-result position.

Top Result Trust

This asks whether the first result is defensible as the best thing to show a customer. A bad #1 is the clearest search failure because it shapes the shopper's first impression.

70%
questionable #1 results

Result

The first result is not reliable enough: 42 of 60 queries (70%) had a weak, partial, or clearly beaten #1 result.

Evidence

  • "Flint adn Tinder": #1 was "No results" (score 0.00). No visible result was available.
  • "need something for outdoor activities": #1 was "No results" (score 0.00). No visible result was available.
  • "Proof sport shorts sale": #1 was "No results" (score 0.00). No visible result was available.
  • "sale shirts under 60": #1 was "No results" (score 0.00). No visible result was available.

How to remedy it

Add a stricter first-result threshold for high-intent queries. If the top result is weak and a stronger result exists lower, boost the stronger exact/category/brand match or suppress the weak partial match from rank #1.

Attribute Drift

This decomposes multi-attribute queries to see which terms survive into the top results. It distinguishes right-category-but-wrong-attribute failures from total search failures.

11
queries with attribute loss

Result

11 multi-attribute queries showed some attribute loss in the top 5. The audit separates preserved, partially preserved, and dropped terms so the team can see which part of the query is being lost.

Evidence

  • "Proof sport shorts sale": dropped proof, sport, short; partially preserved none
  • "chino shorts under 80": dropped chino, short, 80; partially preserved none
  • "Taylor Stitch under 120": dropped taylor, stitch, 120; partially preserved none
  • "exclusive items": dropped exclusive, item; partially preserved none

How to remedy it

Extract query attributes before ranking and give required attributes explicit weight. For brand, size, material, activity, and style terms, penalize results that match the product type but omit or contradict the attribute.

Diversity vs Redundancy

This checks whether broad or exploratory queries show useful choice, or whether the top results are flooded by repeated variants. It is most useful for multi-brand or broad-catalog ecommerce, and less useful for true single-brand stores.

2
unique families in worst broad top 10

Result

The most redundant broad result set was "bestsellers": 2 unique product families across 10 top results.

Evidence

  • "bestsellers": 2 unique product families across 10 top results
  • "shirts": 4 unique product families across 10 top results
  • "performance shorts": 4 unique product families across 10 top results
  • "active": 5 unique product families across 10 top results

How to remedy it

Group color/size/variant duplicates under a product-family cap for broad queries. Keep exact product searches variant-rich, but diversify broad discovery queries across families, brands, styles, and use cases.

Query Specificity Scaling

This compares broad -> specific query chains to see whether search gets better as the shopper becomes clearer. High-specificity queries often signal stronger purchase intent, so degradation here is commercially important.

0
specificity ladders detected

Result

No three-step specificity ladder was detected in this query set. This diagnostic activates when generated queries include broad -> specific query chains.

How to remedy it

Generate intentional specificity ladders during query creation, then use them as regression tests. When a ladder breaks, inspect the attribute added at that step and tune query parsing or ranking weights for that attribute.

Recommendations

Prioritized Roadmap

1

Put query intent rules in front of generic keyword ranking

This gives Product Discovery, Brand Search, Typo Tolerance, and Shopping Context a concrete first layer: route sale and bestseller terms to the right collections, apply price and negative filters, and boost recognized brands or product types before broad keyword matches take over.

Critical Quick Win
2

Implement negative keyword filtering

Fixes the complete failure to handle "not" queries in Filters & Constraints, where customers explicitly excluding items (like "shorts not cargo") still see exactly what they don't want.

Critical Medium Effort
3

Add fuzzy matching for brand names

Resolves Typo Tolerance failures where simple misspellings like "Flint adn Tinder" and "Fahery Brand" return zero results despite clear customer intent.

Critical Medium Effort
4

Configure category mapping for special terms

Fixes Language Understanding issues where queries like "bestsellers" and "sale items" should trigger category pages rather than keyword searches.

Critical Quick Win
5

Implement brand name boosting

Addresses Brand & Model Search failures where brand-specific queries like "Proof shorts" ignore the brand constraint entirely.

Critical Quick Win
6

Add product deduplication logic

Eliminates the duplicate results crowding out better matches, particularly visible in searches like "active" where the same product appears multiple times.

Moderate Medium Effort
7

Build natural language query processing

Enables Language Understanding for conversational queries like "need something for outdoor activities" and "what shirts go with chino shorts" that currently return zero or irrelevant results.

High Major Project
8

Implement price constraint parsing

Fixes Shopping Context failures where price-anchored queries like "shorts under 100" completely ignore the budget constraint.

Moderate Medium Effort
Industry Context

How You Compare

Metric Your Score Industry Target Source
Relevant result in top 3 47% 80%+ Baymard Institute
Average best result position #6 #1–2 Industry best practice
Irrelevant #1 result 29% <10% Industry best practice

70% of ecommerce sites cannot handle typos in their site search (Baymard Institute). Sites that do handle them gain a significant edge.

~30% of ecommerce visitors use site search, but they generate a disproportionate share of revenue — search users convert at 1.8–3x the rate of browse-only visitors (Econsultancy, Algolia).

31% of site searches end at a dead end — irrelevant results or zero results — across the average ecommerce site (Baymard Institute).

The top 3 results capture ~55–60% of all clicks. Results below position 3 are seen by a rapidly shrinking share of customers.

Revenue-Risk Failures

The queries most likely to cost sales

These examples show where the customer either sees no useful result, sees the wrong intent first, or has to dig past weaker results to find the right product.

#1 Flint adn Tinder No fuzzy matching

The best matching result appeared at position #16, after weaker results.

Evidence

Query 'Flint adn Tinder' returned zero results despite containing only a simple typo ('adn' instead of 'and'). The stats show a best result exists at position #16 with displacement of 15 positions, indicating relevant Flint and Tinder products exist in the catalog but the search engine failed to retrieve them due to the typo.

Fix

Add typo and fuzzy matching for high-value product, category, and brand terms so small spelling errors still reach the right products.

Customer saw

Should have seen

#2 need something for outdoor activities Zero results

The best matching result appeared at position #16, after weaker results.

Evidence

The search engine returned zero results for the query 'need something for outdoor activities' despite this being a natural language request that should map to outdoor/active category products. The max relevance score of 0.000 and total results of 0 confirm complete search failure.

Fix

Add query rewriting, token splitting, and fallback matching for this query pattern so customers do not hit a dead end.

Customer saw

Should have seen

#3 Proof sport shorts sale Zero results

The best matching result appeared at position #16, after weaker results.

Evidence

The search returned zero results for the query 'Proof sport shorts sale' despite the query containing valid search terms. The max relevance score was 0.000 and no products were displayed to the customer.

Fix

Add query rewriting, token splitting, and fallback matching for this query pattern so customers do not hit a dead end.

Customer saw

Should have seen

#4 sale shirts under 60 Zero results

The best matching result appeared at position #16, after weaker results.

Evidence

The search returned zero results for 'sale shirts under 60' despite the displacement metric indicating the best result was at position #16, suggesting relevant products exist in the catalog but the search engine failed to retrieve any results at all

Fix

Add query rewriting, token splitting, and fallback matching for this query pattern so customers do not hit a dead end.

Customer saw

Should have seen

#5 spring sale shorts Zero results

The best matching result appeared at position #16, after weaker results.

Evidence

The search returned zero results for 'spring sale shorts' despite this being a straightforward seasonal product query. The stats show 0 total results, max relevance score of 0.000, and the best result was displaced to position 16, indicating the engine found nothing relevant in the top positions.

Fix

Add query rewriting, token splitting, and fallback matching for this query pattern so customers do not hit a dead end.

Customer saw

Should have seen

#6 versatile summer shirts Poor ranking

The best matching result appeared at position #14, after weaker results.

Evidence

The highest relevance result 'Tropical Camo Short Sleeve Shirt' (0.461) was buried at position #14, while the lowest relevance result 'Tellaro Sweater Polo' (0.312) appeared at position #6. The top position was occupied by 'Lousada Camp Collar Shirt' with only 0.381 relevance. Multiple high-scoring summer shirts like 'Palma Linen Short Sleeve Camp Shirt' (0.459) were pushed to positions #11 and #15.

Fix

Re-rank returned products so the strongest intent matches appear first, especially when the right products already exist in the result set.

#2 0.45

Detailed Analysis

Product Discovery

5 examples
"bestsellers"
Broad category · Critical · Best result at position #14

The query 'bestsellers' should map to a bestsellers category/collection, but instead the engine treated it as a keyword search. The top 10 results are dominated by '365 Chino Short - 7"' products (positions #1-9, #11) all with identical relevance scores of 0.363, suggesting keyword matching rather than bestseller ranking. The actual highest-relevance products like 'Women's Silky Gold Omega Chain' (0.414) and 'ConcealPack™ 21L' (0.400) are buried at positions #14 and #13 respectively.

Customer Saw

  1. 365 Chino Short - 7"
  2. 365 Chino Short - 7"
  3. 365 Chino Short - 7"
  4. 365 Chino Short - 7"
  5. 365 Chino Short - 7"

Should Have Seen

  1. Women's Silky Gold Omega Chain
  2. ConcealPack™ 21L
  3. 72-Hour Merino UPF Quarter Zip

Best match was buried at position #14.

"active"
Broad category · Critical · Best result at position #13

The highest relevance result '001 Trail Running Sneaker' (0.480) was buried at position #13, while lower-scoring results dominated the top positions. Multiple duplicate 'Sevens Sport Short Lined - 7"' entries (0.449) occupied positions #1, #3, #9, #10, and #12, and 'PatinaTech T-Shirt' (0.316) appeared at positions #2 and #8 despite having much lower relevance scores.

Customer Saw

  1. Sevens Sport Short Lined - 7"
  2. PatinaTech T-Shirt
  3. Sevens Sport Short Lined - 7"
  4. 005 Trail Sneaker
  5. Wiley REC Polarized Sunglasses

Should Have Seen

  1. 001 Trail Running Sneaker
  2. Sevens Sport Short Lined - 7"
  3. Sevens Sport Short Lined - 7"

Best match was buried at position #13.

"casual wear"
Category mapping · Critical · Best result at position #13

The highest relevance results were buried deep in rankings: Transport Casual Jacket (0.621 relevance) appeared at positions #13 and #14, while much lower relevance items like Fino Pique Polo (0.428) and Women's Stacked Diamond Huggie Earrings (0.361) occupied the top positions. The best matching result was displaced by 12 positions.

Customer Saw

  1. Fino Pique Polo
  2. Fino Pique Polo
  3. Women's Stacked Diamond Huggie Earrings
  4. OTD Graphic T-Shirt
  5. OTD Graphic T-Shirt

Should Have Seen

  1. Transport Casual Jacket
  2. Transport Casual Jacket
  3. OTD Graphic T-Shirt

Best match was buried at position #13.

"swim shorts"
Category mapping · Critical · Best result at position #10

The highest relevance products (Classic Swim Short - 5.5" with 0.844 relevance) were buried at positions #10, #12, #13, and #15, while lower relevance products (Classic Solid Swim Short and Resort Swim Short with 0.836 relevance) dominated positions #1-#9. The best matching result was displaced by 9 positions.

Customer Saw

  1. Classic Solid Swim Short - 5.5"
  2. The Resort Swim Short - 5"
  3. The Resort Swim Short - 5"
  4. The Resort Swim Short - 5"
  5. Classic Solid Swim Short - 5.5"

Should Have Seen

  1. Classic Swim Short - 5.5"
  2. Classic Swim Short - 5.5"
  3. Classic Swim Short - 5.5"

Best match was buried at position #10.

"sale items"
Category mapping · Critical · Best result at position #1

For query 'sale items', the engine returned promotional content instead of actual sale products: 'All Ways Forward' (rank #2, relevance 0.277) and 'Get Free Shipping Today' (rank #3, relevance 0.359) appear to be marketing pages or banners rather than discounted products. Only one actual product appeared: 'norda 001 RZ 2023 Trail Running Sneaker' (rank #1, relevance 0.410), but there's no indication this is a sale item. The engine failed to map 'sale items' to the site's Sale category and instead treated it as keywords to match in content.

Customer Saw

  1. norda 001 RZ 2023 Trail Running Sneaker
  2. All Ways Forward
  3. Get Free Shipping Today

Should Have Seen

  1. norda 001 RZ 2023 Trail Running Sneaker
  2. Get Free Shipping Today
  3. All Ways Forward

Typo Tolerance

5 examples
"Flint adn Tinder"
Typo · Critical · Best result at position #16

Query 'Flint adn Tinder' returned zero results despite containing only a simple typo ('adn' instead of 'and'). The stats show a best result exists at position #16 with displacement of 15 positions, indicating relevant Flint and Tinder products exist in the catalog but the search engine failed to retrieve them due to the typo.

Customer Saw

Should Have Seen

Best match was buried at position #16.

"365 Chin"
Partial query · Critical · Best result at position #12

The highest relevance result '365 Chino Short - 5' (0.676) was buried at position #12, while lower relevance pants dominated the top positions. The top 3 positions showed pants with relevance scores of 0.582, 0.586, and 0.617, while shorts with better scores (0.668, 0.676) were pushed down to positions #7, #11, and #12. This is particularly problematic since the query '365 Chin' appears to be targeting the 365 Chino Short line based on the test rationale.

Customer Saw

  1. 365 Chino Pant - HB Straight™
  2. 365 Chino Pant - HB Slim™
  3. 365 Chino Pant - HB Athletic Tapered™
  4. 365 Chino Pant - HB Athletic Straight™
  5. 365 Chino Pant - HB Athletic Straight™

Should Have Seen

  1. 365 Chino Short - 5"
  2. 365 Chino Short - 7"
  3. 365 Chino Short - 7"

Best match was buried at position #12.

"Tayler Stitch"
Typo · Critical · Best result at position #11

The highest relevance result 'The Stevens Linen Herringbone Blazer' (0.320) was buried at position #11, while lower-scoring results like 'The Jack Everyday Oxford Shirt' (0.277) and 'The Ojai Chore Coat' (0.262) occupied the top positions. The best result was displaced by 10 positions despite having a significantly higher relevance score.

Customer Saw

  1. The Jack Everyday Oxford Shirt
  2. The Ojai Chore Coat
  3. The Surplus Short - 7"
  4. The Surplus Short - 7"
  5. The Valencia Sweater Polo

Should Have Seen

  1. The Stevens Linen Herringbone Blazer
  2. The Jack Everyday Oxford Shirt
  3. The Jack Everyday Oxford Short Sleeve Shirt

Best match was buried at position #11.

"Flywei"
Partial query · Critical · Best result at position #11

The search engine correctly retrieved all relevant Flyweight products but ranked them incorrectly. The highest relevance products (Flyweight Flex Chino Pant with 0.527 relevance) were buried at positions #11, #13, and #14, while lower relevance products dominated the top positions. Specifically, Flyweight Flex Short products with only 0.486 relevance occupied 6 of the top 10 positions, including #1, #4, #7, #8, #9, and #10.

Customer Saw

  1. Flyweight Flex Short - 8.5"
  2. Flyweight Flex Blazer
  3. Flyweight Flex Blazer
  4. Flyweight Flex Short - 8.5"
  5. Flyweight Flex Blazer

Should Have Seen

  1. Flyweight Flex Chino Pant
  2. Flyweight Flex Chino Pant
  3. Flyweight Flex Chino Pant

Best match was buried at position #11.

"Fahery Brand"
Typo · Moderate · Best result at position #7

Customer searched for 'Fahery Brand' (missing 't' in Faherty) but got generic product results with no brand recognition. The highest relevance score was only 0.334 for 'All Day 4-Way Stretch Short - 9'' at position #7, and none of the top results appear to be from Faherty Brand. The search engine failed to recognize the typo and match it to the correct brand name.

Customer Saw

  1. Palma Linen Short Sleeve Camp Shirt
  2. Hunting Island Shirt
  3. Hunting Island Shirt
  4. Inlet Knit Blazer
  5. Palmetto Shirt

Should Have Seen

  1. All Day 4-Way Stretch Short - 9"
  2. Inlet Knit Blazer
  3. Breeze Polo

Best match was buried at position #7.

Language Understanding

5 examples
"need something for outdoor activities"
Natural language · Critical · Best result at position #16

The search engine returned zero results for the query 'need something for outdoor activities' despite this being a natural language request that should map to outdoor/active category products. The max relevance score of 0.000 and total results of 0 confirm complete search failure.

Customer Saw

Should Have Seen

Best match was buried at position #16.

"linen button up"
Synonym · Critical · Best result at position #13

The highest relevance result 'Classic Linen Shirt' (0.629) was buried at position #13, while much lower relevance results dominated the top positions: 'Linen Applique Short Sleeve Shirt' (0.520) at #1, and 'Gonzo Short Sleeve Shirt' (0.305) at positions #2 and #3. The top 3 average relevance was only 0.376, indicating poor ranking quality.

Customer Saw

  1. Linen Applique Short Sleeve Shirt
  2. Gonzo Short Sleeve Shirt
  3. Gonzo Short Sleeve Shirt
  4. Tropical Trap Linen Blazer
  5. Tropical Trap Linen Blazer

Should Have Seen

  1. Classic Linen Shirt
  2. Tropical Trap Linen Blazer
  3. Tropical Trap Linen Blazer

Best match was buried at position #13.

"cargo pants"
Synonym · Critical · Best result at position #12

The highest relevance result 'Ripstop Regiment Cargo Pant' (0.883) was buried at position #12, while lower-scoring results like 'M-51 Lightweight Cargo Pant - Exclusive' (0.875) occupied the top 3 positions. Multiple products with identical relevance scores (0.875, 0.871, 0.867) were scattered throughout the results instead of being grouped together by score.

Customer Saw

  1. M-51 Lightweight Cargo Pant - Exclusive
  2. Utility Cargo Pant
  3. M-51 Lightweight Cargo Pant - Exclusive
  4. Drawstring Cargo Pant - Exclusive
  5. Drawstring Cargo Pant - Exclusive

Should Have Seen

  1. Ripstop Regiment Cargo Pant
  2. Utility Cotton Ripstop Cargo Pant
  3. Cotton Poplin Cargo Pant

Best match was buried at position #12.

"chinos"
Synonym · Critical · Best result at position #10

The two highest relevance results (Cotton Linen Chino - HB Straight™ with 0.770 relevance) were buried at positions #10 and #13, while lower-scoring 365 Chino Pant variants (0.738 relevance) dominated positions #1-2. The 72-Hour Merino Chino variants (0.754 relevance) were also pushed down to positions #11-12 despite outscoring the top-ranked results.

Customer Saw

  1. 365 Chino Pant - HB Straight™
  2. 365 Chino Pant - HB Straight™

  2. Cotton Linen Chino - HB Straight™

Best match was buried at position #10.

"looking for versatile casual shorts"
Natural language · Critical · Best result at position #5

Customer searched for 'versatile casual shorts' but got sandals ranked #1 and #2 (Biarritz Sandal, relevance 0.195). The only potentially relevant result 'All Ways Forward' (which could be shorts) was buried at position #5 despite having the highest relevance score of 0.293. The engine appears to have matched on the word 'short' in 'Journey Short Sleeve Shirt' but completely missed that the customer wanted shorts as a garment category, not shirts.

Customer Saw

  1. Biarritz Sandal
  2. Biarritz Sandal
  3. Journey Short Sleeve Shirt
  4. Journey Short Sleeve Shirt
  5. All Ways Forward

Should Have Seen

  1. All Ways Forward
  2. Journey Short Sleeve Shirt
  3. Journey Short Sleeve Shirt

Best match was buried at position #5.

4 examples
"Flint and Tinder"
Brand search · Critical · Best result at position #10

The three highest-relevance Flint and Tinder products were buried deep in results: 'Flint and Tinder x Rancourt & Co. Leather Tote' (0.766 relevance) at position #10, 'Flint and Tinder x Rancourt & Co. Acadia Chukka Boot' (0.723 relevance) at position #8, and 'Flint and Tinder x Rancourt & Co. Beef Roll Penny Loafer' (0.707 relevance) at position #9. Meanwhile, products with much lower relevance scores dominated the top positions: '365 5-Pocket Pant - HB Athletic Tapered™' (0.199 relevance) at #1, 'Vintage Soft Wash T-Shirt' (0.206 relevance) at #2, and 'Dobby Camp Shirt' (0.236 relevance) at #3.

"Proof shorts"
Brand search · Critical · Best result at position #1

Customer searched for 'Proof shorts' but zero Proof brand products appear in the results. Instead, the engine returned shorts from other brands: Equator Hybrid Short (#1), Sevens Sport Short Lined (positions #2, #4, #7-15), TrailTech Short (#5, #11), and Rover EDC Short (#3, #6). The search engine completely ignored the brand constraint 'Proof' and only matched on the product type 'shorts'.

  4. Sevens Sport Short Lined - 7"

  2. TrailTech Short - 7"
  3. TrailTech Short - 7"
"Taylor Stitch"
Brand search · Minor · Best result at position #2

All results are Taylor Stitch products showing the brand constraint is working, but ranking is suboptimal. The highest relevance product 'The Jack Seersucker Shirt' (0.324) was ranked #2 instead of #1. Higher-scoring items like 'The Jack Everyday Oxford Short Sleeve Shirt' (0.309) and 'The Button Down Polo' (0.309) were buried at positions #6 and #12 respectively, while lower-scoring duplicates of 'The Jack Everyday Oxford Shirt' (0.307) occupied multiple top positions (#1, #3, #5).

Price anchored · Critical · Best result at position #16

The search returned zero results for 'sale shirts under 60' despite the displacement metric indicating the best result was at position #16, suggesting relevant products exist in the catalog but the search engine failed to retrieve any results at all

The highest relevance products '7" Training Short' (0.867 relevance) were buried at positions #11-13, while lower relevance products dominated the top positions. Specifically, 'Drawstring Canoe Short - 7"' products with only 0.824 relevance occupied positions #1-3, and 'Cotton Linen Chino Short - 7"' products with 0.855 relevance were mixed throughout positions #4-9. "shirts not linen"
Negative intent · Critical · Best result at position #10

The query 'shirts not linen' explicitly excludes linen shirts, but 14 out of 15 results are linen shirts: positions #1-9 and #11-15 all show 'Short Sleeve Isles Linen Shirt' or 'Isles Linen Shirt'. Only position #10 'Tencel Resort Shirt' correctly excludes linen material. The best non-linen result is buried at position #10.

"shorts not cargo"
Negative intent · Critical · Best result at position #6

The query 'shorts not cargo' explicitly excludes cargo items, but cargo shorts dominate the results: '365 Cargo Short - 9%' appears at positions #2, #3, #4, '365 Cargo Short - 7%' at positions #10, #11, and 'Cargo Compression Tights' at position #12. Meanwhile, non-cargo shorts like 'Vidda Pro Lite Shorts' (highest relevance 0.523) are buried at positions #6 and #9.

Shopping Context

"spring sale shorts"
Seasonal / occasion · Critical · Best result at position #16

The search returned zero results for 'spring sale shorts' despite this being a straightforward seasonal product query. The stats show 0 total results, max relevance score of 0.000, and the best result was displaced to position 16, indicating the engine found nothing relevant in the top positions.

The top position was occupied by 'Lousada Camp Collar Shirt' with only 0.381 relevance. Multiple high-scoring summer shirts like 'Palma Linen Short Sleeve Camp Shirt' (0.459) were pushed to positions #11 and #15.

"short"
Plural / singular · Critical · Best result at position #11

The two highest relevance products 'Cord Supply Short - 7.5"' (0.719 relevance) were ranked at positions #11 and #12 instead of #1 and #2. The current #1 result 'Sevens Sport Short Lined - 7"' has only 0.691 relevance, which is 0.028 points lower than the best available results. Multiple products with identical relevance scores (0.703, 0.680, 0.676) are scattered throughout the rankings instead of being grouped together.

"premium outdoor shorts"
Subjective attribute · Critical · Best result at position #11

The engine matched only on 'outdoor' and ignored 'shorts' and 'premium'. Results #1-9 are all outdoor furniture sectionals (Felloe Sectional 6-piece, 4-piece, 5-piece, and Terra Outdoor Sectional) with relevance scores 0.230-0.264. The actual shorts products (Baggies Shorts - 5" and Women's Terrebonne Shorts) with much higher relevance scores (0.398-0.410) were buried at positions #10-14.

  1. Felloe Sectional (6-piece)
  2. Felloe Sectional (4-piece)
  3. Felloe Sectional (4-piece)
  4. Felloe Sectional (5-piece)
  5. Felloe Sectional (6-piece)

  2. Baggies Shorts - 5"
  3. Baggies Shorts - 5"

Best match was buried at position #11.

"outdoor adventure gear"
Seasonal / occasion · Critical · Best result at position #11

The highest relevance result 'Outdoor Graphic T-Shirt' (0.516) was buried at position #11, while a much lower relevance result 'Screen-Free Digital Camera' (0.348) was ranked #1. Additionally, the same product 'DB2 - Indestructible Waterproof BT Speaker' appears 6 times consecutively in positions #4-#10, all with identical relevance scores of 0.385, indicating duplicate results are crowding out better matches.

Customer Saw

  1. Screen-Free Digital Camera
  2. Ergo Grip Active Glove
  3. Rambler 20 oz Tumbler
  4. DB2 - Indestructible Waterproof BT Speaker
  5. Huckberry Rambler 20oz Tumbler

Should Have Seen

  1. Outdoor Graphic T-Shirt
  2. Rover Rock Short - 7"
  3. Ergo Grip Active Glove

Best match was buried at position #11.

Positive Findings

What's Working

  • Retrieval is working — relevant results were found in 21 of 60 queries. The primary issue is ranking, not finding: the right results exist in the result set but aren't being surfaced at the top.
  • Close to correct on 21 queries ("The Apres Short - 7"", "365 Chino Short - 7"", "Flyweight Flex Short - 8.5""): the best result was within the top 3, just not at #1.

Scope and Boundaries

What this audit covers

Included

Search relevance, ranking quality, retrieval gaps, query interpretation failures, visible result ordering, and prioritized fixes based on the tested query set.

Not included

Implementation work, platform configuration changes, merchandising strategy, analytics instrumentation, or a full conversion-rate analysis beyond the search result experience.

Next Steps

We'd welcome the opportunity to walk you through these findings in detail and discuss a remediation plan tailored to your team's priorities and timeline.

The issues identified in this audit are fixable — most ecommerce search platforms support the capabilities tested here, and the roadmap above is ordered by impact to help you prioritize.

Schedule a 30-Minute Walkthrough
